title: Enjoy Shawarma Opens Second Arkansas Location on Cantrell Road in West Little Rock

seo.title: Enjoy Shawarma Opens on Cantrell Road in West Little Rock | Metro Little Rock News
seo.description: Enjoy Shawarma opened at 14524 Cantrell Road in West Little Rock, bringing chicken and beef shawarma, falafel, and gyros to the Pinnacle Station corridor.

summary: Enjoy Shawarma opened its second Arkansas location Monday at 14524 Cantrell Road in West Little Rock, in the Pinnacle Station strip center. The Bryant-based concept serves chicken and beef shawarma, falafel, gyros, and hummus. The Cantrell Road opening brings a sit-down Mediterranean option to a stretch of road that has depended mostly on chains.

body_sections.body: Enjoy Shawarma opened Monday at the Pinnacle Station strip center at 14524 Cantrell Road in West Little Rock, marking the concept's first location inside the city of Little Rock. Owner Zeyad Abdelhadi launched the brand in Bryant, where the restaurant built a following on a menu of chicken and beef shawarma, falafel, gyros, and hummus prepared to traditional Mediterranean standards.

body_sections.heading: The Concept
body_sections.body: The menu is built around a small number of core items executed tightly — meats sliced to order from the vertical spit, falafel made from scratch, and a short list of sides and sauces. It operates as a fast-casual concept, which makes it a natural fit for weekday lunch traffic. The Bryant location developed a consistent following since opening, and the Cantrell Road spot is positioned to draw from the dense residential population along the Chenal corridor.

body_sections.heading: Neighborhood Context
body_sections.body: Pinnacle Station at 14524 Cantrell sits in a pocket where West Little Rock transitions toward the Chenal Valley corridor. The strip is a serviceable commercial development — a mix of casual dining and service tenants — without much distinguishing it before this opening. The nearest cluster of non-chain dining options is several miles in either direction. A fast-casual Mediterranean concept landing there addresses a practical gap, not a trendy one.

impact_note: For residents along Cantrell between Rodney Parham and Chenal Parkway, this is a new non-chain lunch option in a stretch of road that didn't have one. The broader pattern is worth noting: several Mediterranean and shawarma concepts have opened across west Little Rock in the past 18 months. More competition in the category tends to improve quality across the board, so the timing of each new entrant is worth watching.
